title: Cyclanthera longisepala

This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other species within the family. intro: Cyclanthera longisepala, also known as the Chinese snake gourd, is a type of vining plant belonging to the Cucurbitaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Cyclanthera longisepala include cucurbitacins, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Cyclanthera longisepala is often used to make tea, soups, and infusions, and it is also used in the preparation of tinctures and decoctions.
